user_message: Summarize what happened with Wide World Importers and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-21 12:50 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers territory planning

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 13:17 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 15:48 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers territory planning

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-03-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Priya (Wide World Importers)

[00:01:30] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into territory planning.

[00:06:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:10:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:15:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:19:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:24:00] Priya (Wide World Importers): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:27:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:30:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Wide World Importers: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF14-A trap (Invents contacts to fill role slots); produces complete Stakeholder map with roles (decision-maker, influencer, champion, blocker), recency labels, confiden
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF14-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF14-A failure mode (Invents contacts to fill role slots)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF14-A -- Invents contacts to fill role slots
